tasks.title: Audit CLI source against spec and produce gap manifest
tasks.description: Audit CLI source against spec and produce gap manifest
tasks.body:
Read every command registration in cmd/ and compare against docs/specs/2026-03-12T00-06Z-cli-commands.md. Produce a gap manifest listing:

1. Commands present in the CLI but absent from the spec (currently identified: audit enrich, orchestrator criteria, task amend).
2. Flags present in the CLI but absent from the spec (currently identified: task add missing --body/--stdin/--deliverable/--type/--class/--constraint/--acceptance/--reference/--integration/--parent; project create missing --type/--description/--scope; spec create missing --body/--stdin; status missing --expand).
3. The updated command count (currently 39 in the spec header; should be at least 42 after additions).

Write the manifest as a checklist in the task markdown file so subsequent tasks can use it as a reference. Cross-reference each gap against the actual Go source to confirm flag names, types, defaults, and descriptions.

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in Spec Gap Analysis is complete and correct

breadcrumbs.text: Completed CLI spec gap analysis: found 3 missing commands (audit enrich, orchestrator criteria, task amend), 16 missing flags across 4 commands, 2 value discrepancies in status --interval, and 2 header updates needed. Total: 23 gaps documented in task-0001.md manifest.

Audit reviewed task-0001.md gap manifest (110 lines) against all cmd/ source files and docs/specs/2026-03-12T00-06Z-cli-commands.md. PASS. Verified all 23 gaps: 3 missing commands (audit enrich, orchestrator criteria, task amend) confirmed in source and absent from spec; 16 missing flags across 4 commands verified by reading flag registrations in add.go, create.go, spec.go, register.go; 2 status --interval discrepancies confirmed (default 2 vs spec 5, missing -n shorthand); 2 header updates confirmed (count 39→43, missing orchestrator group). Reverse gap check found no spec entries without corresponding source. Build clean, all tests pass. No code changes in this node, only the deliverable manifest. Quality: systematic, accurate analysis with clear checklist format suitable for downstream tasks.

audit.result_summary: Spec gap analysis identified 23 discrepancies between cmd/ source (43 leaf commands) and docs/specs/2026-03-12T00-06Z-cli-commands.md (40 documented commands, header claiming 39). Gaps include 3 entirely missing commands (audit enrich, orchestrator criteria, task amend), 16 missing flags across task add, project create, spec create, and status, 2 value discrepancies in status --interval, and 2 stale header fields. All gaps are documented as a checklist in task-0001.md for downstream implementation tasks to consume. Audit verified every claim against source and found no additional gaps or reverse gaps.

audit.objective: Verify the spec gap analysis deliverable is complete, accurate, and ready for downstream consumption
audit.what_happened: Read task-0001.md manifest and cross-referenced every claim against source files: cmd/audit/enrich.go, cmd/orchestrator/criteria.go, cmd/task/amend.go, cmd/task/add.go, cmd/project/create.go, cmd/spec.go, cmd/daemon/register.go. Counted 43 leaf commands in source against 40 in spec. Performed a reverse gap check (spec entries without source) and found none. Ran build and full test suite, both clean.
went_well: The manifest's checklist format with source file references makes verification straightforward
The command count arithmetic (40 documented + 3 missing = 43 in source) is internally consistent and externally verified
improvements: None identified; the manifest is well-structured and complete
action_items: Downstream tasks should process the checklist items in task-0001.md to update the spec document

task-0001.objective: Audit every CLI command registration against the spec and produce a gap manifest
task-0001.what_happened: Read all 43 command registrations in cmd/ via source exploration. Compared each against docs/specs/2026-03-12T00-06Z-cli-commands.md, which documents 40 commands despite its header claiming 39. Found 3 entirely missing commands, 16 missing flags across 4 commands, and 2 value discrepancies in status --interval (default and shorthand).
went_well: Systematic comparison caught the --interval default mismatch (spec says 5, source says 2) which was not in the original task description
The orchestrator command group itself was identified as missing from the spec header group list
improvements: Could have also checked for commands in the spec that no longer exist in source (reverse gap check), though none were found incidentally
action_items: Subsequent tasks should use the checklist in task-0001.md to add each missing command/flag to the spec
